title: A Canine Space Explorer Returns Home
story: Bark if you're in space! On March 25, 1961, a brave dog named Zvezdochka blasted off aboard Sputnik 10. This furry cosmonaut orbited Earth, paving the way for human space travel. Unlike earlier animal astronauts, Zvezdochka safely returned home. Scientists learned valuable lessons about how living creatures cope in zero gravity. Her mission helped ensure humans could soon reach for the stars!
activity_question: What was the name of the spacecraft that carried the dog into orbit?
bonus_fact: Zvezdochka means 'little star' in Russian, a fitting name for a space-traveling pup!

title: Zooming into the Future: Concorde's First Sonic Boom
story: Whoosh! In 1970, a sleek aircraft called Concorde broke the sound barrier for the first time. This amazing plane zipped through the sky at an incredible 700 miles per hour on March 25. Its distinctive pointed nose and delta wings became iconic symbols of aviation innovation.

title: Titan Revealed: Saturn's Mysterious Moon
story: Peering through his telescope, astronomer Christiaan Huygens made an exciting discovery in 1655. He spotted a large, bright object orbiting Saturn - the planet's biggest moon, Titan! On March 25 of that year, Huygens became the first person to observe this mysterious world. Titan is special because it's the only moon in our solar system with thick clouds and liquid on its surface. It's like a mini-planet of its own!
activity_question: What makes Titan unique among moons in our solar system?
activity_choices: It's the only moon with thick clouds and liquid on its surface
It's the largest moon of Jupiter
activity_answer: It's the only moon with thick clouds and liquid on its surface
follow_up_question: Can you guess what kind of liquid might be on Titan's surface?

title: A Giant Eye for the Stars
story: A massive glass disk, destined to become the world's largest telescope mirror, began an epic journey in 1936. This giant 'eye' would help unlock secrets of distant galaxies and expand our understanding of the universe.

title: Tree Huggers Save the Forest
story: Gaura Devi stood firm, arms wrapped around a tree. In 1974, this brave woman from Reni Village in India's Western Himalayas sparked a movement. On March 25, loggers arrived to chop down the forest. But Gaura and other villagers hugged the trees, refusing to let them be cut. This peaceful protest, called Chipko, means to embrace in Hindi. It grew into a powerful way to protect forests across India.

follow_up_question: Why do you think hugging trees was an effective way to protect them?

title: A Celestial Visitor Zooms Past Earth

title: Etna's Fiery Fury Reshapes Sicily
story: The ground shook as Mount Etna roared to life. On March 25, 1669, Europe's largest volcano unleashed its fury on Sicily. Lava flowed down Etna's slopes, engulfing the town of Nicolosi. Today, Etna remains active, closely monitored by scientists to help keep nearby communities safe.

title: Spanish Basketball Pioneer Shoots for the Stars
story: Fernando Martín dribbled his way into history. Born in Madrid on March 25, 1962, he became a basketball sensation. Martín's talent took him all the way to America, where in 1986 he made a giant leap as the first Spaniard to play in the NBA for the Portland Trail Blazers. But that wasn't his only claim to fame. In 1981, he helped Real Madrid clinch the FIBA Club World Cup.
activity_question: What team did Fernando Martín play for in the NBA?
follow_up_question: What other major achievement did Martín have with Real Madrid?

title: Blind Blues-Rock Star Born
story: A musical prodigy entered the world in Toronto, Ontario. Jeff Healey, born in 1966, would become a renowned blind blues-rock guitarist and singer. Despite losing his sight as a baby, Healey developed a unique guitar-playing style, laying the instrument flat on his lap. His talent shone through hits like 'Angel Eyes', inspiring music lovers on March 25 and beyond.
activity_question: What unique guitar-playing style did Jeff Healey develop?
activity_choices: Laying the instrument flat on his lap
activity_answer: Laying the instrument flat on his lap
follow_up_question: How do you think Jeff Healey's unique playing style might have helped him as a blind musician?

title: Greek Revolution Sparks Freedom Fight
story: Freedom fighters raised their voices in 1821! The Greek people officially declared their revolution against Ottoman rule on March 25. Though battles had already begun, this bold announcement marked a turning point. Greeks were determined to win back their independence after centuries of foreign control. Their courage inspired many and set the stage for a long struggle toward liberty.
activity_question: What did the Greek people officially declare on March 25, 1821?
activity_choices: A national day of feta cheese
follow_up_question: Why do you think the Greeks wanted independence?

title: The First Easter Celebration
story: Imagine a special day that would be celebrated for centuries! According to calendar-maker Dionysius Exiguus, Christians celebrated the very first Easter in the year 31. This joyous occasion marked the beginning of a tradition that continues to this day, with March 25 holding a significant place in Christian history.
activity_question: Who said the first Easter was celebrated in the year 31?
follow_up_question: What does Easter celebrate in the Christian faith?
